bugs.error_message: fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git
bugs.root_cause: The requested project directory is not initialized as a git worktree, so git status cannot be used for concurrency inspection.
bugs.fix: Verified the filesystem layout directly and avoided git-dependent workflow assumptions for this scaffold pass.

bugs.error_message: [ERR_PNPM_IGNORED_BUILDS] Ignored build scripts: esbuild@0.25.12, esbuild@0.28.1; [WARN] The "pnpm" field in package.json is no longer read by pnpm.; [ERROR] duplicated mapping key in pnpm-workspace.yaml allowBuilds
bugs.root_cause: pnpm 11 blocks dependency build scripts unless explicitly approved, and pnpm 11 now reads pnpm-specific settings from pnpm-workspace.yaml instead of package.json.
bugs.fix: Kept a single allowBuilds.esbuild=true mapping in pnpm-workspace.yaml and removed pnpm's generated placeholder duplicate.

bugs.error_message: rtk: rtk find does not support compound predicates or actions (e.g. -not, -exec). Use `find` directly.
bugs.root_cause: The RTK wrapper blocks compound find predicates, and the discovery command attempted a multi-name search through find.
bugs.fix: Switched discovery to `rtk rg --files -g ...` and simple RTK-safe commands.

bugs.error_message: error TS6059: File 'apps/server/vitest.config.ts' is not under 'rootDir' 'apps/server/src'.
bugs.root_cause: The app tsconfig used rootDir=src while including package-level config files outside src.
bugs.fix: Limited app tsconfig include patterns to src/**/* so runtime builds only compile source entrypoints.

bugs.error_message: packages/doc-canonical/src/index.ts(39,27): error TS18047: 'value' is possibly 'null'.; packages/doc-canonical/src/index.ts(39,33): error TS2339: Property 'normalize' does not exist on type 'string | number | boolean'.
bugs.root_cause: The canonicalizer handled null, number, boolean, and string in one branch while calling string-only normalize through optional chaining.
bugs.fix: Split primitive handling so only strings are normalized and null/number/boolean are stringified directly.

bugs.root_cause: Server shared/config modules were added without their runtime and type dependencies declared in the server package manifest.
bugs.fix: Added the imported server shared-module dependencies and @types/pg to apps/server/package.json.

bugs.error_message: rtk test -f package.json printed shell usage and exited 2
bugs.root_cause: The RTK wrapper does not pass the shell builtin `test` form through as intended in this environment.
bugs.fix: Use `rtk proxy sh -c 'test -f ...'` or RTK-safe file listing commands for existence checks.

bugs.error_message: src/config/env.ts(16,26): error TS2769: No overload matches this call.
bugs.root_cause: The env secret validator used an ad-hoc ctx annotation while zod's real RefinementCtx type was available after dependencies resolved.
bugs.fix: Imported RefinementCtx from zod and restored z.ZodIssueCode.custom for the superRefine issue.

bugs.error_message: vp lint --deny-warnings scanned node_modules and emitted thousands of third-party warnings.
bugs.root_cause: The root lint script did not constrain Oxlint's path set, so Vite+ lint traversed generated and dependency directories.
bugs.fix: Changed lint to target apps/packages/e2e/tools/vite.config.ts and explicitly ignore dist and node_modules.

bugs.error_message: playwright.config.ts TS2769: Type 'number | undefined' is not assignable to type 'string | number'.
bugs.root_cause: With exactOptionalPropertyTypes, assigning workers: undefined is not valid for PlaywrightTestConfig.
bugs.fix: Changed the CI workers setting to a conditional object spread so the property is omitted outside CI.

bugs.error_message: Playwright browserType.launch: Executable doesn't exist; NO_COLOR env is ignored due to FORCE_COLOR.
bugs.root_cause: Playwright browsers had not been installed after updating @playwright/test, and the inherited terminal color environment produced Node warnings.
bugs.fix: Installed chromium/firefox/webkit with Playwright and changed the E2E script to unset FORCE_COLOR and NO_COLOR before running tests.

bugs.error_message: apps/web check: Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to SetStateAction of localized literal types; locale unit test expected en for ['en-US','ja-JP'] but received ja.
bugs.root_cause: Localized message literals narrowed useState too aggressively, and locale detection checked for any Japanese language instead of respecting browser preference order.
bugs.fix: Widened editable state values to string and changed locale detection to use the first supported language in navigator.languages order.

bugs.error_message: Japanese E2E strict mode violation: getByLabel('文書タイトル') resolved to both the article and input.
bugs.root_cause: The editor article used aria-labelledby pointing at the document title field label, causing duplicate accessible names.
bugs.fix: Removed the article aria-labelledby so the field label uniquely names the input.

bugs.error_message: The core online multi-user editing behavior was not covered by E2E tests.
bugs.root_cause: The first E2E suite focused on shell editing, panels, localization, and viewport checks but did not model two active collaborators.
bugs.fix: Added BroadcastChannel/localStorage draft sync in the web scaffold and a two-page E2E case that verifies title and body edits propagate between active collaborators.

bugs.error_message: Document decoration behavior was not covered by E2E tests.
bugs.root_cause: The editor scaffold used a plain text input flow, so rich-text commands such as bold, italic, underline, and highlight had no user-facing control or test coverage.
bugs.fix: Changed the document body to a contenteditable editor, added formatting toolbar controls, and verified bold/italic/underline/highlight output in Playwright.

bugs.error_message: apps/web test emitted ExperimentalWarning: localStorage is not available because --localstorage-file was not provided.
bugs.root_cause: The React component read browser storage during useState initialization while being rendered through react-dom/server in the unit test.
bugs.fix: Initialized from localized fallback state and moved persisted draft loading into a browser-only effect with guarded storage access.

bugs.error_message: E2E strict mode violation: getByLabel('Format') resolved to both the formatting toolbar and export format select.
bugs.root_cause: The new aria-label 'Formatting toolbar' overlapped the export label 'Format' for Playwright label lookup.
bugs.fix: Scoped the export format selector to the side panel before selecting and asserting the value.

bugs.error_message: PDF export failed in the browser with @pdf-lib/fontkit Error: Unknown op: 0.
bugs.root_cause: The first PDF implementation embedded a WOFF/CFF Noto Sans CJK font that fontkit could load but could not subset reliably.
bugs.fix: Switched PDF embedding to a TTF Noto Sans JP asset from @expo-google-fonts/noto-sans-jp and kept export generation lazy-loaded.

bugs.error_message: semgrep scan --config .semgrep.yml failed with ValueError: unroll_dict called but object was actually str.
bugs.root_cause: Registry rulesets such as p/owasp-top-ten were incorrectly listed as strings under the local YAML rules key.
bugs.fix: Kept local YAML for path exclusions only and moved registry rulesets into the root security:owasp CLI command.

bugs.error_message: Playwright export E2E intermittently read another test's Markdown export output.
bugs.root_cause: Parallel E2E tests shared the same fixed test-results/exports/decorated-export.md path.
bugs.fix: Changed export download validation to write under testInfo.outputPath('exports') for each test case.

bugs.error_message: vite build emitted a chunk size warning after adding PDF/DOCX export dependencies.
bugs.root_cause: The web app statically imported export libraries, so pdf-lib/docx/font assets were included in the initial application chunk.
bugs.fix: Moved exporter loading behind dynamic import, split lightweight rich-text sanitization into its own module, and set a deliberate export chunk warning limit.

bugs.fix: Added guard clause: if (!text)

bugs.fix: Changed to "pnpm --filter='

bugs.fix: Added type assertion/annotation

bugs.fix: Added guard clause: if (typeof override === "string" && override)

bugs.fix: Rewrote 4→3 lines (2 removed)

bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.fix: Added type assertion/annotation

bugs.root_cause: revision: current.revision + 1 → nextRevision(current.revision)
bugs.fix: Changed revision: current.revision + 1 → nextRevision(current.revision)

bugs.root_cause: Missing or incorrect type annotation
bugs.fix: Added type assertion/annotation

bugs.error_message: Vite dev server flooded with `ws proxy error: ECONNRESET/EPIPE` once browsers held WS open through the /ws/draft proxy.
bugs.root_cause: Vite's HTTP proxy keeps trying to forward to dead upstream sockets when a WebSocket session lingers across client/server lifecycle events. The proxy added a fragile hop between browser and ws server with no benefit since both run locally.
bugs.fix: Removed the /ws/draft proxy. Client now connects directly to ws://localhost:3000 in dev (overridable via VITE_COLLAB_WS_URL) and same-origin in production.

bugs.error_message: Cross-browser collab: edits from one browser were not reflected in the other (asymmetric — Brave→Chrome worked, Chrome→Brave did not).
bugs.root_cause: Per-client revision counter started from each browser's localStorage and incremented locally (`current.revision + 1`). When Brave's stored revision was higher than Chrome's, Chrome's incoming drafts had smaller revisions and were filtered out by `next.revision > current.revision`.
bugs.fix: Introduced `nextRevision(current, now)` = max(current + 1, Date.now()). Anchoring on the wall clock keeps revisions globally monotonic enough for last-write-wins, so a fresh peer never silently shadows the other's edits. Exported and unit-tested.

bugs.error_message: No automated regression coverage for the cross-browser WS path; previous 'smoke test' (one-off node script) gave false confidence.
bugs.root_cause: The WS broadcast was only verified by an interactive node REPL script run once. CI never reran it; the test was not portable across sessions.
bugs.fix: Added 5 vitest integration tests in apps/server/tests/draft-collab.test.ts covering relay (no echo), presence count up/down, snapshot for late joiners, unknown-path rejection, and malformed-message tolerance. Runs in `pnpm -r test`.

bugs.root_cause: https: " → " ? "wss:" : "ws:"
bugs.fix: Changed https: " → " ? "wss:" : "ws:"

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: req.url !== path
bugs.fix: Added guard clause: if (req.url !== path)

bugs.root_cause: Missing or incorrect type annotation
bugs.fix: Added type assertion/annotation

bugs.root_cause: revision: typeof parsed.revision === "number" ? parsed.revision : fallback.revision, → fallback.revision,
bugs.fix: Changed revision: typeof parsed.revision === "number" ? parsed.revision : fallback.revision, → fallback.revision,

bugs.root_cause: Missing or incorrect type annotation
bugs.fix: Added type assertion/annotation

bugs.root_cause: 10 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 36→46 lines (10 removed)

bugs.error_message: Cross-origin WS to localhost:3000 was being blocked/throttled in the browser; ECONNRESET/EPIPE flood on Vite proxy.
bugs.root_cause: Two separate failure modes for cross-origin WS in dev: Vite's HTTP proxy churned EPIPE on long-lived sockets, and Brave/Chromium shields can drop cross-origin localhost ws upgrades.
bugs.fix: Workspace-export attachDraftCollab from @wysiwyg-collab-editor/server via package `exports`. Vite plugin in apps/web/vite.config.ts calls attachDraftCollab on Vite's own httpServer so the browser only ever sees same-origin ws://localhost:5173/ws/draft. Also added navigateFallbackDenylist for /ws/ in VitePWA.

bugs.error_message: Both browsers stuck in infinite reload loop after enabling same-origin WS.
bugs.root_cause: attachDraftCollab's `upgrade` listener called `socket.destroy()` for any path != /ws/draft. This destroyed Vite's HMR upgrade socket on the SAME http server, killing HMR and forcing the browser into a full reload loop.
bugs.fix: Replace `socket.destroy()` with a silent `return` for non-matching paths so coexisting listeners (Vite HMR) can claim the socket. Updated regression test 'does not destroy upgrades on unknown paths so coexisting handlers (Vite HMR) keep working'.

bugs.error_message: Edits never propagated — publish() silently no-op'd because socketRef.current was null.
bugs.root_cause: React StrictMode mounts effects twice. The first effect's socket close event fires AFTER the second effect has installed a new socket in socketRef. Unguarded `socketRef.current = null` in the stale onclose handler clobbered the live socket; subsequent publish() saw readyState !== OPEN and dropped every keystroke.
bugs.fix: Identity-check in onclose (`if (socketRef.current === ws) socketRef.current = null`). Removed null-clearing from cleanup; we rely on the identity check instead. Verified end-to-end in two MCP-driven browser tabs.

bugs.error_message: Even after socket fix, peer edits were filtered out as 'older' because each browser's localStorage carried a session-local revision number.
bugs.root_cause: loadStoredDraft restored the persisted `revision` from localStorage. A tab that had edited heavily in an earlier session loaded a huge revision; the peer's fresh `Date.now()`-based edits had a smaller revision and were silently dropped by the `next.revision > current.revision` filter.
bugs.fix: loadStoredDraft now drops the persisted revision and always uses the fallback's revision. Revisions are session-local; only title+bodyHtml are persisted. Added a unit test pinning the regression: stored revision 9_999_999_999_999 must NOT survive load.

bugs.error_message: Comments never propagated to peers — adding a comment in one tab never appeared in another.
bugs.root_cause: Comments were a field on DraftState. Last-write-wins on the draft meant any peer broadcasting a draft update with no comments (or fewer comments) would clobber the partner's list. Aggravated by Node module caching in the Vite plugin: changes to attachDraftCollab in apps/server/src/main.ts don't take effect until Vite is restarted.
bugs.fix: Split comments out into a dedicated append-only event stream (type 'comment' from client; server stores in commentsById Map; broadcasts to ALL peers including sender for idempotent reconciliation; new joiners get a 'comments' snapshot). Client now has independent `comments` useState, separate from DraftState. Server validation tightened (id non-empty, text trimmed non-empty, createdAt finite). Server tests added for cross-peer relay, dedup by id, snapshot for late joiner, malformed payload rejection. Verified live in two browser tabs with sync in both directions.
